architecture.summary: The Hyperdimensional Probe is a hybrid supervised probe that maps LLM residual stream embeddings into Vector Symbolic Architecture (VSA) encodings via a trained neural encoder. The pipeline: (1) extracts embeddings from LLM forward passes, (2) compresses via k-means clustering and sum pooling to reduce dimensionality, (3) trains a shallow feedforward MLP (55M–71M parameters) to map compressed embeddings to bipolar hypervectors, and (4) queries the VSA proxy space using hypervector unbinding operations to extract atomic concepts. Information flows from token embeddings through dimensionality reduction, then through the neural VSA encoder into a bounded, queryable symbolic space where concepts are retrieved via algebraic operations.

biblio.title: Hyperdimensional Probe: Decoding LLM Representations via Vector Symbolic Architectures

claims.dataset: LLM-VSA dataset (70-15-15 train-val-test split of analogy corpus)
claims.statement: Neural VSA encoder achieves test-set average cosine similarity of 0.88 between predicted and target VSA encodings
Neural VSA encoder achieves average binary accuracy of 0.96 on binarized VSA encodings

claims.dataset: analogy test set (verbose template, 114,099 distinct examples)
claims.statement: VSA-based probing achieves average precision@1 of 83% in extracting target concepts from LLM embeddings across all models
VSA probing extracts target concepts with their keys in 76% of cases on average (KeyTarget category), compared to DLA producing no concepts in 29.5% of analogies
In instances where DLA produced no concepts (29.5% of cases), VSA probing extracted the key-target pair in 57.4% of instances

claims.dataset: analogy test set across 44 domains, 5 categories
claims.statement: VSA-based concept extraction reveals varying conceptual richness across models and input types: larger models (OLMo-2) extract richer concept sets including context and target together (4.4%), while smaller models (GPT-2) rely more on input-related concepts (22% extract no concepts). Mathematical analogies show highest rate of empty representations (88%), while morphological analogies show lowest (1.1%).

claims.dataset: SQuAD (693,886 training examples constructed via bundling operations)
claims.statement: On SQuAD question-answering task, neural VSA encoder achieves test-set cosine similarity of 0.44 and binary accuracy of 0.70 for Llama 3.1-8B

Largest model evaluated (Llama 4 Scout, 109B parameters) exhibited lowest precision@1 in next-token prediction (8%), even underperforming legacy GPT-2-medium, despite strong probing performance, indicating discrepancy between internal concept encoding and output generation likely caused by tokenization and prompt design factors
